forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: df80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Focus bar: make the indicator thinner (4px → 2px)'
+---
+## What
+
+The focus-bar indicator (the vertical pill painted on every `[data-focused]` element via `::before`) is too thick — currently `width: 0.25rem` (= 4px). Halve it to `width: 0.125rem` (= 2px) so it's a clearer "indicator line" and less visually heavy on dense UIs like the data-table grid.
+
+### Single change site
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/index.css:159` — the `[data-focused]::before` rule:
+
+```css
+[data-focused]::before {
+  content: "";
+  position: absolute;
+  left: -0.5rem;
+  top: 0.125rem;
+  bottom: 0.125rem;
+  width: 0.25rem;  /* ← change to 0.125rem */
+  border-radius: 9999px;
+  background-color: var(--primary);
+  box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 50%, transparent);
+}
+```
+
+### Why only one site
+
+The three override rules (`.column-header-focus[data-focused]::before`, `.mention-pill-focus[data-focused]::before`, `.data-table-header-focus[data-focused]::before` at index.css:166-191) all override `left` only; they inherit `width` from the base rule. One change covers every consumer.
+
+### Optional polish
+
+Consider introducing a CSS variable — `--focus-bar-width: 0.125rem` — so future tweaks don't require hunting through the cascade. This is a judgment call; the current codebase uses literal values for one-off tokens, so keeping a literal is fine unless the implementer sees value in the variable.
+
+### No tests to update
+
+Grep confirms no existing test asserts on `getPropertyValue("width")` or `0.25rem` for focus-bar styling. This is a pure visual tweak.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `[data-focused]::before` rule in `kanban-app/ui/src/index.css` has `width: 0.125rem`
+- [ ] All focus-bar callsites (cards, grid cells, row selectors, LeftNav buttons, perspective tabs, inspector rows, column headers, mention pills) visually show a thinner bar
+- [ ] `border-radius: 9999px` still produces a pill shape (2px wide + rounded looks fine — verify visually)
+- [ ] No regression in the `.column-header-focus`, `.mention-pill-focus`, or `.data-table-header-focus` positioning — their `left` overrides still apply correctly
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Manual verification in the running app:
+  - Focus a board card → bar appears, visibly thinner than before
+  - Focus a grid cell → same
+  - Focus a row selector → same
+  - Focus a LeftNav button → same
+  - Focus a perspective tab → same
+  - Focus an inspector field → same
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all 1301+ existing tests still pass (no test asserts on bar width, but the change could not break anything anyway)
+- [ ] If the implementer chooses to introduce `--focus-bar-width`, add a brief inline comment noting that all override rules inherit width from the base
+
+## Workflow
+
+- One-line CSS change. No TDD needed — the change is visual and the assertion is "user approves after seeing it."
+- If the user reports the bar is now too thin or the user wants a specific pixel value, file a follow-up with the exact target.
+

changes.value: Focus bar: make the indicator thinner (4px → 2px)

## What

The focus-bar indicator (the vertical pill painted on every `[data-focused]` element via `::before`) is too thick — currently `width: 0.25rem` (= 4px). Halve it to `width: 0.125rem` (= 2px) so it's a clearer "indicator line" and less visually heavy on dense UIs like the data-table grid.

### Single change site

`kanban-app/ui/src/index.css:159` — the `[data-focused]::before` rule:

```css
[data-focused]::before {
  content: "";
  position: absolute;
  left: -0.5rem;
  top: 0.125rem;
  bottom: 0.125rem;
  width: 0.25rem;  /* ← change to 0.125rem */
  border-radius: 9999px;
  background-color: var(--primary);
  box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 50%, transparent);
}
```

### Why only one site

The three override rules (`.column-header-focus[data-focused]::before`, `.mention-pill-focus[data-focused]::before`, `.data-table-header-focus[data-focused]::before` at index.css:166-191) all override `left` only; they inherit `width` from the base rule. One change covers every consumer.

### Optional polish

Consider introducing a CSS variable — `--focus-bar-width: 0.125rem` — so future tweaks don't require hunting through the cascade. This is a judgment call; the current codebase uses literal values for one-off tokens, so keeping a literal is fine unless the implementer sees value in the variable.

### No tests to update

Grep confirms no existing test asserts on `getPropertyValue("width")` or `0.25rem` for focus-bar styling. This is a pure visual tweak.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `[data-focused]::before` rule in `kanban-app/ui/src/index.css` has `width: 0.125rem`
- [ ] All focus-bar callsites (cards, grid cells, row selectors, LeftNav buttons, perspective tabs, inspector rows, column headers, mention pills) visually show a thinner bar
- [ ] `border-radius: 9999px` still produces a pill shape (2px wide + rounded looks fine — verify visually)
- [ ] No regression in the `.column-header-focus`, `.mention-pill-focus`, or `.data-table-header-focus` positioning — their `left` overrides still apply correctly

## Tests

- [ ] Manual verification in the running app:
  - Focus a board card → bar appears, visibly thinner than before
  - Focus a grid cell → same
  - Focus a row selector → same
  - Focus a LeftNav button → same
  - Focus a perspective tab → same
  - Focus an inspector field → same
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all 1301+ existing tests still pass (no test asserts on bar width, but the change could not break anything anyway)
- [ ] If the implementer chooses to introduce `--focus-bar-width`, add a brief inline comment noting that all override rules inherit width from the base

## Workflow

- One-line CSS change. No TDD needed — the change is visual and the assertion is "user approves after seeing it."
- If the user reports the bar is now too thin or the user wants a specific pixel value, file a follow-up with the exact target.

+The three override rules (`.column-header-focus[data-focused]::before`, `.mention-pill-focus[data-focused]::before`, `.data-table-header-focus[data-focused]::before` at index.css:166-191) all override `left` only; they inherit `width` from the base rule. One change covers every consumer.
+
+### Optional polish
+
+Consider introducing a CSS variable — `--focus-bar-width: 0.125rem` — so future tweaks don't require hunting through the cascade. This is a judgment call; the current codebase uses literal values for one-off tokens, so keeping a literal is fine unless the implementer sees value in the variable.
+
+### No tests to update
+
+Grep confirms no existing test asserts on `getPropertyValue("width")` or `0.25rem` for focus-bar styling. This is a pure visual tweak.
+

+

+- [ ] All focus-bar callsites (cards, grid cells, row selectors, LeftNav buttons, perspective tabs, inspector rows, column headers, mention pills) visually show a thinner bar
+- [ ] `border-radius: 9999px` still produces a pill shape (2px wide + rounded looks fine — verify visually)
+- [ ] No regression in the `.column-header-focus`, `.mention-pill-focus`, or `.data-table-header-focus` positioning — their `left` overrides still apply correctly
+
